pr.title: Allow `wiki_tu.py` to update existing wiki page
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the Translation Units wiki generation tooling so `tools/wiki_tu.py` can read an existing wiki markdown page, preserve existing Discord/GitHub assignee values, and regenerate the table with current objdiff report data. The PR added `mistletoe` as a Markdown parser dependency, introduced a `read` helper/subcommand for testing wiki parsing, added an optional existing wiki path to the `write` command, and made a small executable shebang fix to `tools/diff_changes.py`.

key_files.role: Main tooling change: parses existing Translation Units wiki markdown and reuses assignee data during table regeneration.
Added `mistletoe` as a misc tooling dependency.

what_changed: `tools/wiki_tu.py` now imports `mistletoe` and token classes to parse Markdown tables instead of treating the existing wiki as plain text.

Added `read_wiki(lines) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]`, which walks a `mistletoe.Document`, finds `Table` and `TableRow` nodes, strips HTML comments from raw cell text, and records rows with non-empty Discord or GitHub assignee cells.
Added a `read` subcommand for testing markdown parsing; it reads from a wiki path or stdin and pretty-prints the parsed assignee mapping.

Extended the `write` subcommand with an optional `WIKI` path argument. When provided, it reads the existing Translation Units wiki page and carries over assignee information into the newly generated output.
Changed output generation to keep a separate `file_link` for display while using the stripped source path as the lookup key for assignees.

When assignee data exists, Discord usernames are emitted as backticked plaintext and GitHub usernames are emitted as links to `../commits?author=<username>`; otherwise the previous HTML placeholders `<!-- Discord -->` and `<!-- GitHub -->` are preserved.
Updated the generated wiki instructions to say users can write usernames as plaintext and the script will format them later.
smart_moves: Used a real Markdown parser (`mistletoe`) to recover table contents, which is less brittle than ad hoc pipe-splitting for wiki markdown tables.
Preserved human-maintained wiki assignee state while still allowing the generated table metrics to be refreshed from the objdiff JSON report.
Stripped HTML placeholder comments when reading raw text, allowing empty placeholder cells to remain non-assignments instead of becoming literal assignee values.
Kept the generated file link separate from the lookup key, avoiding mismatches caused by using rendered markdown links as map keys.
Added a lightweight `read` subcommand to test and debug parsing behavior independently from full wiki generation.
decomp_lessons: For generated project-status pages that developers manually edit, tooling should round-trip human-maintained fields instead of overwriting them on every regeneration.
Markdown table parsing should use a parser when preserving wiki state; generated pages often contain links, inline code, HTML comments, and formatting that make simple string splitting fragile.
Keep stable semantic identifiers, such as source-relative file paths, separate from presentation formatting, such as markdown links or code spans.
Placeholder comments can be useful in generated wiki tables, but readers must explicitly strip or ignore them when determining whether a cell has real user data.
Adding a read-only/test mode to generation scripts makes future maintenance safer because parsing can be validated without modifying output.
naming_conventions: The script uses subcommands named `read` and `write` for markdown parsing diagnostics and report-to-wiki generation.
Existing wiki assignee state is represented as `Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]`, keyed by file path with nested `discord` and `github` fields.
Unassigned generated cells continue to use `<!-- Discord -->` and `<!-- GitHub -->` placeholders.
GitHub assignees are formatted as links to `../commits?author=<github_username>` while Discord assignees are formatted as inline code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code matching tactics were involved; this PR was focused on Python tooling for maintaining the Translation Units wiki.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to `tools/wiki_tu.py` that adjust mistletoe parsing or Translation Units wiki formatting.
Search for PRs touching `reqs/misc.in` or `reqs/misc.txt` to see whether `mistletoe` version pinning changed.
Look for automation that invokes `wiki_tu.py write` with the optional existing wiki path.
Check whether the `read` subcommand remained a test/debug interface or was later removed or expanded.
